events.text: Ashley Perez, 71-year-old Hispanic female, Current smoker (1 ppd). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Adenocarcinoma of the Right upper lobe of lung. Clinical staging: T3N2M0, AJCC Stage IIIA. ICD-10: C34.11. Stage III non-small cell lung cancer with EGFR L858R mutation.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 2 rash (acneiform). Treated with doxycycline 100 mg BID and topical clindamycin gel.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 elevated transaminases. ALT 95 U/L, AST 78 U/L. Treatment held one week, then resumed at reduced dose.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 2 rash (acneiform). Treated with doxycycline 100 mg BID and topical clindamycin gel.
PET/CT performed for restaging after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Underwent surgical resection: Right upper lobectomy with mediastinal lymph node dissection. Estimated blood loss 250 mL. Length of stay 7 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated adjuvant targeted therapy: Osimertinib 80 mg daily. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on adjuvant targeted therapy.
Clinical visit for follow-up on adjuvant targeted therapy.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Ashley Perez
MRN: patient_f24ddd4938cc
Date of Study: 2023-07-16

CLINICAL INDICATION: Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Irregular mass in the right upper lobe of lung measuring 4.6 x 3.7 cm. New finding.

MEDIASTINUM: Right hilar lymphadenopathy, largest node measuring 1.7 cm in short axis.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right upper lobe mass consistent with primary lung malignancy, as described.
2. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy, likely nodal metastatic disease.

Electronically signed by: David Kim, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Ashley Perez
MRN: patient_f24ddd4938cc
Specimen ID: S2023-23853
Date of Procedure: 2023-07-17
Date of Report: 2023-07-22

SPECIMEN: Right upper lobe of lung, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Adenocarcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 2.2 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
TTF-1: Positive
Napsin-A: Positive
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
PD-L1 (22C3): TPS 40%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Ashley Perez - Right upper lobe of lung" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 2.2 x 1.5 x 1.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A6.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Michael Torres, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Ashley Perez
MRN: patient_f24ddd4938cc
Date: 2023-07-25
Provider: Dr. Amanda Foster

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Perez is a 71-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung, IIIA. Patient was referred by primary care physician after abnormal imaging. Stage III non-small cell lung cancer with EGFR L858R mutation. Pathology confirmed adenocarcinoma. Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports palpable mass noted by patient.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- obesity
- osteoarthritis
- hyperlipidemia

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Cesarean section x 2 (if applicable)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Current smoker (0.5 ppd)
- Alcohol: Former heavy drinker, quit 5 years ago
- Occupation: Nurse (retired)
- Lives with alone

FAMILY HISTORY:
- No significant family history.

ALLERGIES: Codeine (nausea)

MEDICATIONS:
- Atorvastatin 40 mg daily

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: constipation, mild cough, mild dyspnea on exertion, abdominal bloating. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 144/80, HR 74, RR 15, Temp 98.4F, SpO2 96% on RA, Wt 82.5 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 3.2, Hgb 12.0, Plt 194, Cr 0.76

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
71-year-old female with IIIA adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. initial oncology consultation.

1. Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung, Stage IIIA
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend neoadjuvant chemotherapy
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Priya Sharma, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Ashley Perez
MRN: patient_f24ddd4938cc
Date of Report: 2023-08-01
Specimen: Right upper lobe of lung (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Adenocarcinoma
Panel: Guardant360 CDx (468 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 7.7 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  EGFR p.L858R (c.2573T>G) - Missense (Exon 21) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  RB1 Loss - Deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ATM p.V2424G - VUS
  BRCA2 p.K1003E - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No ALK, ROS1, RET, or NTRK fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - EGFR L858R: FDA-approved targeted therapies include osimertinib (preferred), erlotinib, afatinib, gefitinib.
  - PD-L1 expression should be assessed for immunotherapy eligibility.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 418x coverage.
Tumor content: 50%.

Electronically signed by: Katherine Liu, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

Patient: Ashley Perez

Specimen ID: S2023-97160

Date of Report: 2023-12-07

SPECIMEN: Right upper lobe of lung, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.8 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
TTF-1: Positive
Napsin-A: Positive
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
PD-L1 (22C3): TPS 40%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Ashley Perez - Right upper lobe of lung" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 6.8 x 4.8 x 3.4 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A7.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

BRAIN MRI WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Ashley Perez
MRN: patient_f24ddd4938cc
Date of Study: 2024-11-24

CLINICAL INDICATION: Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Prior study dated approximately 10 weeks ago available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE: MRI performed with and without IV gadolinium contrast, multiplanar multisequence technique.

FINDINGS:
